seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with DSPy + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with DSPy + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with DSPy enables programmatic optimization of LLM pipelines for intent classification, RAG, and multi-turn conversations, moving beyond brittle prompting. However, DSPy's optimized modules can overfit to training data, produce hallucinated reasoning, or fail safety checks in dynamic conversations. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures, ensuring response accuracy, coherence, and safety before user exposure.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with DSPy
architectureOverview.description:
A DSPy-powered AI chatbot typically starts with an IntentClassifier module to route user queries, followed by a Retrieval module using vector search over a knowledge base. The core response generation uses a RAGChain combining retrieved context with ChainOfThought for reasoning. Multi-turn memory is managed via a ConversationBufferMemory module that maintains context across interactions, with safety checks at each forward pass.

DSPy signatures define input/output behavior for each module (e.g., intent -> category, context -> response), optimized via teleprompters like BootstrapFewShot. The pipeline composes these modules into a forward() method that processes user input through intent → retrieve → generate → verify stages.

Verification is critical since optimization can create brittle chains where small input variations cause assertion failures or incoherent outputs.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Input
    ↓
[IntentClassifier] → category
    ↓
[Retriever] → context chunks
    ↓
[CoT+RAG Generator] → response
    ↓
[Memory Manager] → conversation history
    ↓
[Safety Filter] → final output

DSPy Modules: signatures → optimized → forward()
Vex: verify() wraps each forward()

risks.name: Overfitted Intent Classification
risks.description: DSPy optimization creates classifiers that fail on OOD user intents, misrouting medical queries to sales responses
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies intent predictions against ground-truth categories with semantic similarity checks

risks.name: Hallucinated RAG Responses
risks.description: Chain-of-thought produces plausible but factually wrong answers despite retrieved context
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-validates generated responses against retrieved documents using entailment checks

risks.name: Conversation Incoherence
risks.description: Memory module loses context across turns, causing repetitive or contradictory responses
risks.mitigation: Vex checks response coherence against conversation history using continuity assertions

risks.name: Safety/PII Violations
risks.description: Uncaught toxic language or PII leakage in multi-turn conversations
risks.mitigation: Vex applies safety classifiers and PII entity extraction before response delivery

vexIntegration.title: Verify Intent Classification
vexIntegration.description: Wrap intent classifier to catch overfitting failures

vexIntegration.title: RAG Hallucination Detection
vexIntegration.description: Verify responses are grounded in retrieved context
vexIntegration.code:
def verified_rag_forward(self, context: str, question: str) -> dict:
    raw_response = self.generate(context=context, question=question)
    
    verified = vex.verify(
        model_output=raw_response,
        assertions=[
            vex.Assertion(
                name='grounded_response',
                metric=vex.Metric.ENTAILMENT,
                reference=context,
                field='answer',
                threshold=0.8
            ),
            vex.Assertion(
                name='no_hallucination',
                metric=vex.Metric.SIMILARITY,
                reference=question + context,
                field='answer',
                threshold=0.7
            )
        ]
    )
    return verified.output if verified.passed else {'answer': 'Unable to verify response'}

vexIntegration.title: Safety + PII Full Pipeline
vexIntegration.code:
class VerifiedAIChatbot(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.vex_client = vex.Client()
    
    def forward(self, user_query: str, docs: str = '') -> dict:
        # All Vex-wrapped modules
        intent_result = self.verified_intent(user_query)
        rag_result = self.verified_rag(docs, user_query)
        
        # Final safety check
        final_result = vex.verify(
            model_output=rag_result,
            assertions=[
                vex.Assertion(name='no_pii', metric=vex.Metric.NER, 
                            field='answer', threshold=0.0),
                vex.Assertion(name='safety', metric=vex.Metric.TOXICITY, 
                            field='answer', threshold=0.1),
                vex.Assertion(name='coherence', metric=vex.Metric.CONTINUITY,
                            reference=self.memory.get_context(), field='answer')
            ],
            risk_profile='high'
        )
        
        return final_result.output

cta.heading: Secure Your DSPy AI Chatbot
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your DSPy modules today to eliminate production failures.
